requirements.full_sentence: Chapter VI - Financial Conduct and Prohibited Activities .................................................

These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative
Banks - Miscellaneous) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Miscellaneous) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence: These Directions shall come into force with immediate effect.
requirements.object: immediate effect

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to Urban Co-operative Banks (hereinafter
collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank').
requirements.object: Co-operative Banks (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank')

requirements.full_sentence:
In this context, urban co-operative banks shall mean Primary Co-operative Banks
as defined under section 5(ccv) read with Section 56 of Banking Regulation Act,
1949).
requirements.object: as defined under section 5(ccv) read with Section 56 of Banking Regulation Act, 1949)

requirements.full_sentence:
and
(2) 'DICGC' means the Deposit Insurance and Credit Guarantee Corporation
established under Section 3 of the Deposit Insurance Corporation Act, 1961.
requirements.object: Credit Guarantee Corporation established under Section 3 of the Deposit Insurance Corporation Act, 1961

requirements.full_sentence:
All other expressions, unless defined in the individual chapters below, shall have the
same meaning as have been assigned to them under the Reserve Bank of India Act,
1934, or the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, or any statutory modification or re-
enactment thereto, or Glossary of Terms published by the RBI, or as used in
commercial parlance, as the case may be.
requirements.object: as have been assigned to them under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, or the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, or any statutory m

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall have a Board-approved policy for 'Mandatory Leave' as specified in
paragraph 49 of these Directions and the same shall be periodically reviewed and
updated.
requirements.object: for 'Mandatory Leave' as specified in paragraph 49 of these Directions and the same shall be periodically reviewed and up

requirements.full_sentence:
Chapter III - Depositor Education and Awareness Fund
Explanation: A bank shall deposit the amounts to be credited to the Fund in the
specified account maintained with RBI.
requirements.object: be credited to the Fund in the specified account maintained with RBI

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall transfer to the Fund the entire amount as specified in the above
paragraph, including the accrued interest that the bank would have been required to
pay to the customer / depositor as on the date of transfer to the Fund.
requirements.object: the entire amount as specified in the above paragraph, including the accrued interest that the bank would have been required to p
to them below: (1) 'Committee' means the Committee constituted under the Fund
to as the Scheme

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) 'Liquidator' means liquidator of a bank appointed under any law for the time
being in force;
requirements.object: appointed under any law for the time being in force

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) 'Principal amount' means the amount, including interest, transferred by a bank
to the Fund in terms of Section 26A of the Act;
requirements.object: transferred by a bank to the Fund in terms of Section 26A of the Act
any account or any deposit in a bank remaining unclaimed or inoperative for ten years or more

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall credit to the Fund the credit balance in any of the deposit account
maintained with the bank which have not been operated upon for ten years or more,
or any amount remaining unclaimed for ten years or more, as applicable to the
banking entity.
requirements.object: the credit balance in any of the deposit account maintained with the bank which have not been operated upon for ten years or more,

requirements.full_sentence:
Any expenditure incurred for the promotion of depositors' education, awareness,
interests and other purposes that may be specified by RBI under Section 26A (4) of
the Act, shall be charged to the Fund.
requirements.object: Fund

conditions:
in case of deceased
depositors) whose unclaimed amount / deposit had been transferred to the Fund
if applicable
requirements.full_sentence:
In case of demand from a customer / depositor (or legal heirs in case of deceased
depositors) whose unclaimed amount / deposit had been transferred to the Fund, a
bank shall repay the customer / depositor, along with interest if applicable, and lodge
a claim for refund from the Fund for an equivalent amount paid to the customer /
depositor.
requirements.object: depositor, along with interest if applicable, and lodge a claim for refund from the Fund for an equivalent amount paid to the cus

requirements.full_sentence:
The interest payable, if any, from the Fund on a claim shall accrue only from the date
on which the balance in an account was transferred to the Fund to the date of
payment to the customer / depositor.
requirements.object: date on which the balance in an account was transferred to the Fund to the date of payment to the customer / depositor

requirements.full_sentence:
No interest shall be payable in respect of
amounts refunded from the Fund, in respect of which no interest was payable by the
bank to its customer / depositor.
requirements.object: of amounts refunded from the Fund, in respect of which no interest was payable by the bank to its customer / depositor

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall calculate the interest payable (by a bank to its depositors / claimants) on
principal amount of unclaimed interest bearing deposits transferred to the Fund at the
rate of 4 percent per annum up to June 30, 2018, 3.5 percent w.e.f.
requirements.object: (by a bank to its depositors / claimants) on principal amount of unclaimed interest bearing deposits transferred to the

requirements.full_sentence:
July 1, 2018 up
to May 10, 2021 and at 3 percent with effect from May 11, 2021 till the time of payment
to the depositor / claimant.
requirements.object: 3 percent with effect from May 11, 2021 till the time of payment to the depositor / claimant

requirements.full_sentence:
Changes to the rate of interest, if any, payable on the
principal amount transferred to the Fund shall be specified by RBI from time to time.
requirements.object: from time to time

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: The amount of interest payable in this behalf shall be calculated in the
manner specified in paragraph 17 of these Directions and by rounding off the amount
of interest to the nearest rupee.
requirements.object: manner specified in paragraph 17 of these Directions and by rounding off the amount of interest to the nearest rupee

conditions:
if any claim is received from depositors whose deposits were covered
by DICGC insurance at the time of transfer to the Fund
requirements.full_sentence:
In the case of a bank under liquidation, during the pendency of the liquidation
proceedings, if any claim is received from depositors whose deposits were covered
by DICGC insurance at the time of transfer to the Fund, the Fund shall pay to the
liquidator, an amount equal to the amount that could have been claimed from DICGC
with respect to such deposits, and with respect to all other amounts paid by the
liquidator towards the amounts transferred to the Fund, whether insured by DICGC
or not, the Fund shall reimburse the liquidator.
requirements.object: an amount equal to the amount that could have been claimed from DICGC with respect to such deposits, and with respect to all ot

conditions:
If the deposits of a
customer / depositor were covered by DICGC insurance at the time of transfer
to the DEA Fund
requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
Explanation: In the case of a bank under liquidation, the depositor has to approach
the Liquidator of the bank for claim and the Liquidator shall settle the claim as per the
following procedure:
(1) Scenario 1: Claim on Deposits Covered by DICGC - If the deposits of a
customer / depositor were covered by DICGC insurance at the time of transfer
to the DEA Fund, then the Liquidator can claim an amount equivalent to what
could have been claimed from DICGC, and then make payment to the depositor.
requirements.object: per the following procedure: (1) Scenario 1: Claim on Deposits Covered by DICGC - If the deposits of a customer / depositor were c

conditions: If the above deposit amount is more than the insurance cover of DICGC
requirements.full_sentence:
If the above deposit amount is more than the insurance cover of DICGC, then
the Liquidator shall claim the amount in excess of DICGC insurance cover only
on reimbursement basis i.e., the Liquidator shall pay such amount to the
depositor subject to meeting all the applicable requirements and thereafter
submit a claim to DEA Fund for reimbursement.
requirements.object: excess of DICGC insurance cover only on reimbursement basis i

conditions:
if not already registered under the DEA
Fund Module of the e-Kuber system
requirements.full_sentence:
Registration in e-Kuber System: A bank, if not already registered under the DEA
Fund Module of the e-Kuber system, shall expeditiously register itself as a pre-
requisite to remit the unclaimed amounts due and submit refund claims, as defined in
the Scheme, in electronic form through e-Kuber system.
requirements.object: a pre- requisite to remit the unclaimed amounts due and submit refund claims, as defined in the Scheme, in electronic

requirements.full_sentence:
A member bank, i.e., a bank
with direct access to the e-Kuber system, shall share two e-mail ids with
dea.fund@rbi.org.in to complete the registration process while a non-member bank
shall provide two e-mail ids to its sponsor bank to complete the registration process.
requirements.object: with dea

requirements.full_sentence:
On completion of registration process, further communication from RBI shall be sent
only to the two e-mail ids registered in the e-Kuber system.
requirements.object: the two e-mail ids registered in the e-Kuber system

requirements.full_sentence:
Authorised Signatories: A bank shall designate up to a maximum of 10 officers as
authorised signatories to operate the bank's DEA Fund account jointly, who shall be
<image>
responsible for authorising the applicable returns under the DEA Fund Scheme.
requirements.object: maximum of 10 officers as authorised signatories to operate the bank's DEA Fund account jointly, who shall be <image> responsible fo

requirements.full_sentence:
It
shall submit to RBI a certified true copy of the Resolution / Decision / Authorisation
(in Hindi or English) of the Board / MD&CEO / ED / Committee of Executives
empowered for the purpose along with the list of authorised signatories.
requirements.object: certified true copy of the Resolution / Decision / Authorisation (in Hindi or English) of the Board / MD&CEO / ED / Committee of Execu

requirements.full_sentence:
Any update
in the authorised officials shall be furnished in the prescribed format (Annex I), with
details of both Resolution / Decision / Authorisation and specimen signatures of all
the authorised signatories.
requirements.object: prescribed format (Annex I), with details of both Resolution / Decision / Authorisation and specimen signatures of all the authori

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: A bank, while communicating the changes made, shall ensure to submit
details of all such authorised signatories and their specimen signatures to RBI,
instead of furnishing only the additions or deletions made.
requirements.object: of all such authorised signatories and their specimen signatures to RBI, instead of furnishing only the additions or deletion

requirements.full_sentence:
Procedure for Transferring Unclaimed Amount to the Fund: A bank shall transfer
to the Fund, the amounts becoming due in each calendar month (i.e., proceeds of the
inoperative accounts and balances remaining unclaimed for 10 years or more) as
specified in paragraph 9 of these Directions, i.e.
requirements.object: the amounts becoming due in each calendar month (i

requirements.full_sentence:
Before transferring the amount due to the Fund, the bank shall
ensure that all legal obligations relating to the same, till that date, including those
pertaining to taxes deductible and payable, are met or adequate arrangements are
made for the same.
requirements.object: obligations relating to the same, till that date, including those pertaining to taxes deductible and payable, are met or adequat

requirements.full_sentence:
(1) Member Bank's Own Account - A member bank shall transfer to the Fund, the
entire amount due through e-Kuber system under the module "DEA Fund
Services".
requirements.object: the entire amount due through e-Kuber system under the module "DEA Fund Services"

conditions: When a member bank is crediting amount due to the Fund
requirements.full_sentence:
When a member bank is crediting amount due to the Fund, it shall
furnish its DEA Fund Code in the "Bank DEA Fund Code" field and the detailed
breakup (number of accounts and amount) of the deposits, viz., interest bearing
deposits, non-interest bearing deposits and other credits, which also includes
non-interest bearing amount (i.e., any amount other than deposits remaining
unclaimed as mentioned in paragraph 8 of these Directions), in the fields
provided for the same in the e-Kuber system.
requirements.object: Code in the "Bank DEA Fund Code" field and the detailed breakup (number of accounts and amount) of the deposits, viz

conditions:
In case a sponsor bank is remitting
the amounts due of non-member banks
requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Other Bank's (Non-member) Account - In case a sponsor bank is remitting
the amounts due of non-member banks, it shall not consolidate but separately
<image>
remit the amount bank-wise to the Fund, by indicating appropriate Bank DEA
Fund Code of the other (non-member) bank in the field provided in the e-Kuber.
requirements.object: remit the amount bank-wise to the Fund, by indicating appropriate Bank DEA Fund Code of the other (non-member) bank

requirements.full_sentence:
It shall also provide the detailed break-up (number of accounts and amount) of
the deposits, viz., interest-bearing deposits, non-interest bearing deposits and
other credits in the respective fields, i.e., the fields designated for the same, in
the e-Kuber system.
requirements.object: break-up (number of accounts and amount) of the deposits, viz

requirements.full_sentence:
Window for transferring unclaimed amount and submission of claim
(1) Deposit Window: A bank shall ensure correctness of the amount and
transfer unclaimed amounts / deposits to the Fund through e-Kuber during
the last five working days of every month.
requirements.object: amount and transfer unclaimed amounts / deposits to the Fund through e-Kuber during the last five working days of every mont

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank (including a non-member
bank) shall effect only one transfer of unclaimed amounts per month.
requirements.object: of unclaimed amounts per month

requirements.full_sentence:
A non-
member bank shall transfer the unclaimed amounts / deposits to its sponsor
bank (through normal banking channel), sufficiently in advance of the due
date, to enable the sponsor bank to transfer the same to the Fund through e-
Kuber system.
requirements.object: / deposits to its sponsor bank (through normal banking channel), sufficiently in advance of the due date, to enable the

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Claim Window: A bank shall ensure correctness of the amount and submit
claim from the Fund in e-Kuber system during the first 10 working days of
every month.
requirements.object: amount and submit claim from the Fund in e-Kuber system during the first 10 working days of every month

requirements.full_sentence: A bank shall submit only one consolidated claim per month.
requirements.object: claim per month

requirements.full_sentence:
A
non-member bank shall submit the claim to its sponsor bank sufficiently in
advance of the due date, to enable the sponsor bank to submit the same to
RBI through e-Kuber system.
requirements.object: its sponsor bank sufficiently in advance of the due date, to enable the sponsor bank to submit the same to RBI through e-Kuber sys

conditions: In case of demand from a customer / depositor
if applicable
requirements.full_sentence:
Procedure for Submitting Claim
(1) In case of demand from a customer / depositor, whose unclaimed amount /
deposit had been transferred to the Fund, a bank shall repay the customer /
depositor, along with interest, if applicable, and thereafter, lodge a claim for
refund from the Fund for an equivalent amount paid to the customer / depositor.
requirements.object: depositor, along with interest

conditions:
In case of any claim made by the customer / depositor for refund of only part
amount

if any) in the
account
requirements.full_sentence:
In case of any claim made by the customer / depositor for refund of only part
amount, the bank shall repay the customer accordingly by making the account
<image>
operative and keep the remaining amount (including the interest, if any) in the
account, and thereafter lodge a claim for the entire amount from the Fund.
requirements.object: by making the account <image> operative and keep the remaining amount (including the interest

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: A bank may refer Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative
Banks - Responsible Business Conduct) Directions, 2025 for operational
guidelines on activation of inoperative accounts.
requirements.object: India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Responsible Business Conduct) Directions, 2025 for operational guidelines on activation of ino

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall submit a printout of the auto generated Form II signed by
the authorised officials and certified by the bank's auditors (internal / concurrent)
to RBI, by e-mail and/or by post, within three working days of its submission on
the e-Kuber system.
requirements.object: the auto generated Form II signed by the authorised officials and certified by the bank's auditors (internal / concurrent) to RBI

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank / non-member bank shall also submit a copy of
the latest half-year Form III (Reconciliation Certificate - Annex VII) and Annual
Certificate (Annex VIII), while submitting the first claim during the half-year /
year, as the case may be, along with the claim form - Form II, else it will result
in non-consideration of claim of the bank.
requirements.object: of the latest half-year Form III (Reconciliation Certificate - Annex VII) and Annual Certificate (Annex VIII), while submitting the

conditions:
In case of claims from the
Fund by a non-member bank
requirements.full_sentence:
In case of claims from the
Fund by a non-member bank, RBI will credit the account of the sponsor bank
and the sponsor bank shall credit the same to the non-member bank.
requirements.object: the non-member bank

requirements.full_sentence:
Therefore, the onus of making correct refund claims from the
Fund shall lie solely with the bank.
requirements.object: bank

conditions:
in case of
refund claims in Form II
requirements.full_sentence:
(5) While a bank is not required to provide the customer-wise details in case of
refund claims in Form II, it shall maintain the customer-wise details of claims at
its end, duly certified by its auditors (internal/concurrent), which RBI may seek
at a later stage / during the supervisory review process.
requirements.object: of claims at its end, duly certified by its auditors (internal/concurrent), which RBI may seek at a later stage / d

requirements.full_sentence:
(6) Proper due diligence as required under Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-
operative Banks - Know Your Customer) Directions, 2025, as amended from
time to time, shall also be carried out before making payments to customers.
requirements.object: before making payments to customers

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall have an appropriate internal operational procedure for the Fund which
should specifically address error prevention mechanisms and rectification processes.
requirements.object: operational procedure for the Fund which should specifically address error prevention mechanisms and rectification proces

requirements.full_sentence:
Accordingly, the bank shall implement a Maker-Checker process to verify all deposit
and claim entries for processing the entries.
requirements.object: to verify all deposit and claim entries for processing the entries

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall immediately report to RBI any errors, including:
(1) specific reasons for the error,
(2) details of checks and controls implemented to prevent recurrence, and
(3) assurance that such errors will not recur.
requirements.object: any errors, including: (1) specific reasons for the error, (2) details of checks and controls implemented to prevent recurre

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall submit in original (unless specified otherwise), the following returns duly
certified by the specified auditors to Depositor Education and Awareness (DEA) Fund,
Department of Regulation, Central Office, 12th Floor, Nariman Bhavan, Vinay K Shah
Marg, Nariman Point, Mumbai - 400021, as also scanned copy in pdf form by email
to dea.fund@rbi.org.in:
(1) Form I - Monthly Statement: At the end of every month, irrespective of transfer
of deposits, the e-Kuber system will auto generate a Form I (Annex II) for a
bank (including a non-member bank) and send it to their registered e-mail ids.
requirements.object: specified otherwise), the following returns duly certified by the specified auditors to Depositor Education and Awareness (

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank (or sponsor bank on behalf of its non-member bank), after verifying the
correctness of Form I, shall submit the same online to RBI through e-Kuber
system.
requirements.object: to RBI through e-Kuber system

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Rectification Form: If a bank (sponsor bank in case of non-member bank) does
not agree with the balances given in the Form I with regard to the details of
transfers made / claims received including non-receipt of confirmation
messages, then it shall bring the same to the notice of RBI by submitting the
relevant rectification form duly signed by the two authorised officials and
<image>
certified by the bank's auditors (internal / concurrent), by post and/or email,
within two weeks from identification of such discrepancy.
requirements.object: the notice of RBI by submitting the relevant rectification form duly signed by the two authorised officials and <image> certified by

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall submit its
rectification requests in the prescribed forms, as under:
requirements.object: in the prescribed forms, as under:

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Form III - Reconciliation Certificate: A bank shall, for independent and
periodical verification of the balances, at the end of March and September every
year, prepare and keep on record a Reconciliation Certificate (RC) - Form III
(Annex VII) signed by two senior officers, other than those involved in transfer
and refund claims for unclaimed deposits, and countersigned by the bank's
auditors (internal / concurrent), certifying that the balances of the bank as shown
in its general ledger tally with the amount reflected in the Fund account of RBI.
requirements.object: periodical verification of the balances, at the end of March and September every year, prepare and keep on record a Reconciliati

requirements.full_sentence:
This certificate shall be prepared and completed with auditor(s) certification
within a period of one month from the end of every half-year, i.e., April 30 and
October 31 respectively.
requirements.object: with auditor(s) certification within a period of one month from the end of every half-year, i

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall note that submission of a copy of the latest
half-year RC (Form III) to RBI is required, only whenever a first claim of the half-
year is made by the banks and shall be submitted in Form III, which shall contain
Unique Document Identification Number (UDIN) or Internal Document
Identification Number of the bank's auditors (internal / concurrent).
requirements.object: a copy of the latest half-year RC (Form III) to RBI is required, only whenever a first claim of the half- year is made by the

requirements.full_sentence:
To avoid any
kind of avoidable discrepancies in reconciliation of the balances in the Fund, a
bank shall take on record / account the transactions in its books on actual basis,
i.e., only after settlement of claim / transfer of amount from / to the DEA Fund
maintained by RBI.
requirements.object: account the transactions in its books on actual basis, i

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) Annual Certificate by Statutory Auditor: An Annual Certificate (AC) indicating
item-wise details of outstanding amount due at the year-end shall be obtained
by a bank, from its Statutory Auditors in the prescribed format (Annex VIII).
requirements.object: bank, from its Statutory Auditors in the prescribed format (Annex VIII)

requirements.full_sentence:
The
<image>
same shall be submitted to RBI within one month from the date of completion of
bank's Statutory Audit but not later than September 30 of the subsequent
financial year for which the AC pertains to.
requirements.object: within one month from the date of completion of bank's Statutory Audit but not later than September 30 of the subsequent financial

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall furnish the AC, even if
it is a 'NIL' return, to the RBI within the above stipulated period.
requirements.object: if it is a 'NIL' return, to the RBI within the above stipulated period

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall disclose all unclaimed liabilities (where amount due has been transferred
to Fund as also the amounts transferred to Fund in its financial statements and / or
under the Notes to Accounts as specified in Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-
operative Banks - Financial Statements: Presentation and Disclosures) Directions,
2025.
requirements.object: (where amount due has been transferred to Fund as also the amounts transferred to Fund in its financial statements

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall ensure all entries related to Fund are audited pre and post submission,
signed by both authorised signatories and the bank's auditors (internal / concurrent).
requirements.object: to Fund are audited pre and post submission, signed by both authorised signatories and the bank's auditors (internal / conc

requirements.full_sentence:
On the date of transferring the amount to the Fund, a bank should maintain customer-
wise details verified by the concurrent auditors, including payment of up-to-date
interest accrued, that has been credited to the deposit account till the date of transfer
to the Fund, with respect to interest bearing deposits.
requirements.object: verified by the concurrent auditors, including payment of up-to-date interest accrued, that has been credited to the d

requirements.full_sentence:
With respect to non-interest
bearing deposits and other credits transferred to the Fund, customer-wise details,
duly audited, shall be maintained with the bank.
requirements.object: bank

requirements.full_sentence:
The concurrent auditors shall also
verify and certify that, as per the banks' books, the returns have been correctly
compiled by the bank in the monthly and yearly returns submitted to RBI.
requirements.object: that, as per the banks' books, the returns have been correctly compiled by the bank in the monthly and yearly returns submitte

requirements.full_sentence:
The above
returns shall also be verified by the statutory auditors at the time of annual audit.
requirements.object: the statutory auditors at the time of annual audit

requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding anything contained in the Banking Companies (Period of
Preservation of Records) Rules, 1985, a bank shall preserve records or documents
containing details of all accounts and transactions, including deposit accounts in
respect of which amounts are required to be credited to the Fund permanently;
requirements.object: containing details of all accounts and transactions, including deposit accounts in respect of which amounts are required

conditions: where refund has been claimed from the Fund
requirements.full_sentence:
and
where refund has been claimed from the Fund, a bank shall preserve records or
documents in respect of such accounts and transactions, for a period of at least five
years from the date of refund from the Fund.
requirements.object: in respect of such accounts and transactions, for a period of at least five years from the date of refund from the Fund

conditions:
If called upon by RBI or the Committee as defined at paragraph 7(1) of these
Directions
requirements.object: amount due to the Fund

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall duly furnish the updated contact details (in case of any change) by e-
mail to dea.fund@rbi.org.in in the prescribed format (Annex IX) for any
correspondence with RBI relating to DEA Fund Scheme.
requirements.object: contact details (in case of any change) by e- mail to dea

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank may refer to the Scheme notified in the Official Gazette on May 24, 2014 for
other details.
requirements.object: notified in the Official Gazette on May 24, 2014 for other details

conditions: If any issue arises in the interpretation of the provisions of the Scheme
requirements.full_sentence:
If any issue arises in the interpretation of the provisions of the Scheme,
the matter shall be referred to RBI, and the decision of RBI thereon shall be final.
requirements.object: and the decision of RBI thereon shall be final

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank authorised to undertake forex business as either Authorised Dealer Category
I or II, may participate, as a client, in designated currency futures exchanges
recognized by SEBI subject to RBI (Foreign Exchange Department) guidelines in the
matter, only for the purpose of hedging its underlying exposures.
requirements.object: in designated currency futures exchanges recognized by SEBI subject to RBI (Foreign Exchange Department) guidelines in the m

requirements.full_sentence:
The banks may also facilitate the process of activation of such
accounts by taking an empathetic view as indicated in Circular
DoS.CO.PPG.SEC.12/11.01.005/2024-25 dated December 2, 2024.
requirements.object: of activation of such accounts by taking an empathetic view as indicated in Circular DoS

conditions:
in case of banks coming under the purview of Mumbai
Office) of RBI for grant of no objection certificate (NOC)
requirements.full_sentence:
A bank desirous of change in its name shall approach the concerned Regional
Office (DoR, Central Office in case of banks coming under the purview of Mumbai
Office) of RBI for grant of no objection certificate (NOC), clearly stating the
reason(s) for such change (as per format given in Annex X) along with the approval
of the General Body of the bank.
requirements.object: Office (DoR, Central Office in case of banks coming under the purview of Mumbai Office) of RBI for grant of no objecti

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanations:
(1) A bank shall submit request for change of name and bye-laws, only when
these are supported by valid and compelling reason/s for the proposed
change.
requirements.object: of name and bye-laws, only when these are supported by valid and compelling reason/s for the proposed change

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) RBI shall have the discretion to assess whether the reason(s) submitted by
the bank are valid and compelling.
requirements.object: assess whether the reason(s) submitted by the bank are valid and compelling

conditions:
in case there is a requirement for 'confirmation' from Central /
State Government
requirements.full_sentence:
Under section 49C read with section 56 of BR Act, 1949, an NOC from RBI shall
be necessary only in case there is a requirement for 'confirmation' from Central /
State Government, one or more authority / authorities for alteration of bye-laws
under the applicable Co-operative Act / Rules.
requirements.object: case there is a requirement for 'confirmation' from Central / State Government, one or more authority / authorities for alteratio

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall approach the Central Registrar of Co-operative Societies (CRCS) or
Registrar of Co-operative Societies (RCS), as the case may be, for amendment in
its bye-laws after getting NOC from the concerned office of RBI.
requirements.object: of Co-operative Societies (CRCS) or Registrar of Co-operative Societies (RCS), as the case may be, for amendment in its

requirements.full_sentence:
Once the approval
is obtained from CRCS / RCS, the bank shall apply to the concerned Regional
Office of RBI with the following documents:
(1) approval of Board of Directors;
requirements.object: Regional Office of RBI with the following documents: (1) approval of Board of Directors

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: The CRCS / RCS shall not signify its approval to the change of name of
any co-operative bank unless the Reserve Bank certifies in writing that it has no
objection to such change.
requirements.object: the change of name of any co-operative bank unless the Reserve Bank certifies in writing that it has no objection to such chan

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, no application for the confirmation of the alteration
of bye-laws of a co-operative bank shall be maintainable unless the Reserve Bank
certifies that there is no objection to such alteration.
requirements.object: Reserve Bank certifies that there is no objection to such alteration

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall follow the above process for change in the name of the bank even if
the name change is due to Government Notification.
requirements.object: for change in the name of the bank even if the name change is due to Government Notification

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall not display / operate with amended name without effecting the
corresponding change in its name in the banking licence issued by RBI.
requirements.object: amended name without effecting the corresponding change in its name in the banking licence issued by RBI

requirements.full_sentence:
Further,
the displayed name of the bank shall be strictly as per the name in its banking
licence.
requirements.object: the name in its banking licence

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank may use abbreviations / abridged name / logo etc., so long as the full name
as appearing in the banking licence is also shown along with such abbreviated /
abridged names in all publicity material / stationery.
requirements.object: name / logo etc
one used for its abbreviated name / abridged name / Logo

requirements.full_sentence:
and
(2) the words "co-operative bank", indicating the nature of the bank, must be
prominently displayed in the full name / abbreviation / abridged name/logo of
the bank in equal fonts.
requirements.object: the full name / abbreviation / abridged name/logo of the bank in equal fonts

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: A bank shall generally display its full name in any stationery item,
publicity material, website, mobile application, advertisement, name board, etc.,
and the same must conform to the name as it appears in the Certificate of
Registration granted by the RCS and the banking licence granted by RBI.
requirements.object: name in any stationery item, publicity material, website, mobile application, advertisement, name board, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
However, it may use abbreviations / abridged name / logo etc., as part of its brand
building effort, subject to fulfilling the above requirements.
requirements.object: name / logo etc., as part of its brand building effort

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall ensure that no money transaction of the company(ies), declared as
'defaulted companies' by the Hon'ble Patna High Court vide its order dated April
10, 2007, be permitted.
requirements.object: transaction of the company(ies), declared as 'defaulted companies' by the Hon'ble Patna High Court vide its order dated April 10,

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall not cite or quote from the RBI circular dated April 06, 2018 as it was
set aside by the Hon'ble Supreme Court on March 04, 2020 in the matter of Writ
Petition (Civil) No.528 of 2018 (Internet and Mobile Association of India v.
requirements.object: the RBI circular dated April 06, 2018 as it was set aside by the Hon'ble Supreme Court on March 04, 2020 in the matter of Writ Peti

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: A bank shall, however, continue to carry out customer due diligence
processes in line with regulations governing standards for Know Your Customer
(KYC), Anti-Money Laundering (AML), Combating of Financing of Terrorism (CFT)
and obligations of regulated entities under Prevention of Money Laundering Act,
(PMLA), 2002 in addition to ensuring compliance with relevant provisions under
Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA) for overseas remittances.
requirements.object: carry out customer due diligence processes in line with regulations governing standards for Know Your Customer (KYC), Anti-Mone

requirements.full_sentence: Chapter VI - Financial Conduct and Prohibited Activities

A Mahila bank shall not enrol male members as 'A' category or nominal members
except as nominal members for the purpose of standing as sureties for the borrowers
from the bank.
requirements.object: 'A' category or nominal members except as nominal members for the purpose of standing as sureties for the borrowers from the ban

conditions: in case of partnership firm
requirements.full_sentence:
Further, while enrolling proprietary / partnership firm, company / body
corporate as members, the following conditions shall be complied with:
(1) in case of partnership firm, the majority of shareholding shall be held by
women;
requirements.object: in case of partnership firm, the majority of shareholding shall be held by women

requirements.full_sentence:
and
(2) in respect of company / body corporate, majority of shareholding shall be held
by women.

conditions:
if a Mahila Bank conforms to the extant EPN for
general category banks
requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding the relaxations permitted in the Entry Point Capital Norms (EPN) for
a Mahila bank to the extent of 50 percent of the minimum required capital prescribed
for the general category of banks, if a Mahila Bank conforms to the extant EPN for
general category banks, then it may enrol male members up to a limit of 25 percent
of its total regular membership, subject to compliance by the bank with its bye-laws.
requirements.object: general category of banks

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: Approval of the concerned Registrar of Co-operative Societies / Central
Registrar of Co-operative Societies may be obtained, wherever applicable, with
reference to instructions contained in paragraph 40 and paragraph 41 above.

Provided that, such investments shall be subject to limits and conditions specified
in Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Classification, Valuation
and Operation of Investment Portfolio) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: and conditions specified in Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Classification, Valuation and Operation of Investme

requirements.full_sentence:
Normal donations made during a year by a bank, in aggregate, shall be restricted to
a ceiling of one per cent of the published profits of the bank for the previous year.
requirements.object: ceiling of one per cent of the published profits of the bank for the previous year

requirements.full_sentence:
Such normal donations, together with those that may be made to National Funds and
<image>
other funds recognized / sponsored by the Central / State Government, during a year,
shall not exceed two per cent of the published profits of the bank for the previous
year.
requirements.object: of the published profits of the bank for the previous year

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that, a bank carrying accumulated losses in its balance sheet shall not be
eligible to make donations.
requirements.object: donations

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: The restriction in paragraph 43 shall be applicable to all banks
notwithstanding funds, if any, available in the charity fund or any similar reserve
created under the provision of any other legislation.
requirements.object: banks notwithstanding funds

conditions: where directors
requirements.full_sentence:
A bank is prohibited from giving donations to trusts and institutions, where directors,
and / or their relatives hold positions or are interested, even within the permissible
ceiling specified in paragraph 43 above.
requirements.object: trusts and institutions

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: For the purpose of this paragraph,
(1) A person shall be deemed to be a relative of another, if and only if:
requirements.object: a relative of another

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall note that Ministry of Labour Employment and Rehabilitation had notified
co-operative banks as banking companies for the purposes of Section 2(8) of the
Payment of Bonus Act, 1965 vide notification No.
requirements.object: Labour Employment and Rehabilitation had notified co-operative banks as banking companies for the purposes of Section 2(8) of th

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall not accept deposits at the instance of private financiers / unincorporated
bodies under any arrangement which provides for either the issue of deposit receipts
favouring the clients of private financiers or the giving of authority by power of
attorney, nomination or otherwise for such clients to receive such deposits at maturity.
requirements.object: instance of private financiers / unincorporated bodies under any arrangement which provides for either the issue of deposit rec

requirements.full_sentence:
Accordingly,
association of a bank with the deposit acceptance activities of unincorporated
bodies may lead to violation of statutory provisions.
requirements.object: statutory provisions

requirements.full_sentence:
Pursuant to the Prize Chits and Money Circulation Schemes (Banning) Act, 1978,
which bans the promotion and conduct of prize-chit schemes except by charitable
and educational institutions notified in that behalf by the state governments
concerned, a bank shall desist from participating or associating in such prize-chit
schemes floated by any person, firm or any of the non-banking financial institutions
in any form.
requirements.object: associating in such prize-chit schemes floated by any person, firm or any of the non-banking financial institutions in an

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall not associate itself directly or indirectly with lottery schemes of
organisations of any description.
requirements.object: indirectly with lottery schemes of organisations of any description

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall, as per the Board-approved policy referred to in paragraph 6 above,
prepare a list of sensitive positions to be covered under 'mandatory leave'
requirements and the list shall be reviewed periodically.
requirements.object: Board-approved policy referred to in paragraph 6 above, prepare a list of sensitive positions to be covered under 'mandatory leave' requi

requirements.full_sentence:
As per the mandatory leave policy, the employees posted in sensitive positions or
areas of operation shall be compulsorily sent on leave for a few days (not less than
10 working days) in a single spell every year, without giving any prior intimation to
these employees, thereby maintaining an element of surprise.
requirements.object: leave for a few days (not less than 10 working days) in a single spell every year, without giving any prior intimation to thes

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall ensure that employees, while on mandatory leave, do not have access
to any physical or virtual resources related to their work responsibilities, except
internal / corporate email which is usually available to all employees for general
purposes.
requirements.object: on mandatory leave, do not have access to any physical or virtual resources related to their work responsibilities

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank having negative net worth may approach the concerned Regional Office of
RBI with specific proposal for financial restructuring after it has been approved by
its Board, provided that the proposal conforms to the following norms:
(1) No conversion of deposits into equity shall be permitted in respect of
depositors holding deposits in the bank up to the insurance coverage limit
specified by the Deposit Insurance and Credit Guarantee Corporation
(DICGC) from time to time;
requirements.object: of depositors holding deposits in the bank up to the insurance coverage limit specified by the Deposit Insurance and Credit Gu

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A portion of the deposits of individual depositors exceeding the DICGC
insurance coverage limit may be converted into equity.

Similarly, a portion of
the deposits of institutional depositors may be converted into Innovative
Perpetual Debt Instruments (IPDIs), which are eligible for inclusion in Tier I
capital.
requirements.object: Perpetual Debt Instruments (IPDIs), which are eligible for inclusion in Tier I capital

requirements.full_sentence:
Issuance of such IPDIs shall be governed by the terms and conditions
set out in Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Prudential
Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: terms and conditions set out in Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions,

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that, the conversion of deposits into equity and / or IPDI shall be
subject to consent of the depositors.
requirements.object: of the depositors

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Post-restructuring, no shares (equities) shall be redeemed until the bank
achieves a CRAR as per regulatory requirement.
requirements.object: bank achieves a CRAR as per regulatory requirement

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) The proportion of deposits converted into equity or IPDI shall be such that the
net worth of the bank turns positive after the financial restructuring.
requirements.object: net worth of the bank turns positive after the financial restructuring

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that, this requirement regarding positive net worth shall not apply if
the depositors agree voluntarily for such a conversion.
requirements.object: agree voluntarily for such a conversion

requirements.full_sentence: (5) The bank shall maintain CRR / SLR on the restructured liabilities.
requirements.object: on the restructured liabilities

requirements.full_sentence:
Aspects related to management shall be considered on a case-to-case basis while
considering the financial restructuring proposal.
requirements.object: case-to-case basis while considering the financial restructuring proposal

requirements.full_sentence:
A Salary Earners' / Employees' Co-operative Credit Society classified as a Primary
Co-operative Bank which desires to opt out of the purview of the BR Act shall:
(1) amend its by-laws so as to prohibit acceptance of deposits from non-
members.
requirements.object: by-laws so as to prohibit acceptance of deposits from non- members

requirements.full_sentence:
The amendment shall provide for acceptance of deposits only from
members and the term 'member' will also include such persons as were once
'full-fledged' members of the society but have ceased to be so on retirement
from service and have been admitted as nominal / associate / sympathiser
members in accordance with the provisions to that effect in the by-laws of the
society subject to, and in conformity with, the provisions of the Co-operative
Societies Act / Rules;
requirements.object: deposits only from members and the term 'member' will also include such persons as were once 'full-fledged' members of the s

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) pass a resolution of the executive committee / board of directors that the
society shall forthwith stop accepting deposits from non-members.
requirements.object: from non-members

requirements.full_sentence:
In
pursuance of this, it shall take immediate steps to refund deposits from non-
members within a specified date which may be about two months from the
date of passing the resolution.
requirements.object: refund deposits from non- members within a specified date which may be about two months from the date of passing the resolutio

requirements.full_sentence:
It shall also send notices by registered post to
the non-member depositors stating that it has been decided to convert the
society into a non-banking institution and that the depositors shall obtain
payment of the balances standing to the credit of their deposit accounts within
a period of one month from the date of the notice.
requirements.object: registered post to the non-member depositors stating that it has been decided to convert the society into a non-banking instituti

conditions: If it so chooses
requirements.full_sentence:
If it so chooses, the bank
may send notices to the non-members to receive their deposits back even
before the amendment passed at its general body meeting is duly approved
and registered by the competent authority.
requirements.object: non-members to receive their deposits back even before the amendment passed at its general body meeting is duly approved and regis

requirements.full_sentence:
However, this action shall be taken
only in pursuance of the board's resolution as above.
requirements.object: pursuance of the board's resolution as above

requirements.full_sentence:
It may publish a similar
notice in any one of the local newspapers;
requirements.object: in any one of the local newspapers

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
Provided that, if any deposits from non-members remain unclaimed or unpaid
after the expiry of the period specified in the notice referred to above, the
bank/society shall arrange to deposit an equivalent amount with the central
co-operative bank of the district concerned / state co-operative bank of the
state in a special account to be opened for the specific purpose of paying the
non-member deposits.
requirements.object: equivalent amount with the central co-operative bank of the district concerned / state co-operative bank of the state in a speci

requirements.full_sentence:
Alternatively, the bank/society may earmark
government securities or other trustee securities of such face value, the
market value of which is not less than the amount of its dues to non-member
depositors and lodge such securities with the central co-operative bank / state
co-operative bank.
requirements.object: other trustee securities of such face value, the market value of which is not less than the amount of its dues to non

requirements.full_sentence:
In either case, the action shall be supported by a specific
resolution to that effect by the board / executive committee of the society.
requirements.object: specific resolution to that effect by the board / executive committee of the society

requirements.full_sentence:
After taking the steps as above and after allowing the period specified for refund of
non-member deposits to lapse, the bank/society which had accepted deposits from
non-members, shall apply to the concerned Regional Office (Department of
Regulation) of the Reserve Bank of the state in which the society is situated for
notifying it as a non-banking institution under Section 36A(2) of the Banking
Regulation Act, 1949 (As applicable to Co-operative Societies).
requirements.object: Regional Office (Department of Regulation) of the Reserve Bank of the state in which the society is situated for notifying it a

requirements.full_sentence:
The application
shall be accompanied by the following documents and statements:
(1) A certified true copy of the amendment of the by-laws prohibiting the
acceptance of deposits from non-members together with a copy of the
certificate of registration of the amendment from the competent authority.
requirements.object: following documents and statements: (1) A certified true copy of the amendment of the by-laws prohibiting the acceptance of depo

requirements.full_sentence:
If
the amendment of the by-laws provides for acceptance of deposits from
nominal or associate or sympathiser members, a certified true copy of the
provisions in the Co-operative Societies Act or the Rules under the Act
regarding nominal or associate members may also be furnished;

in which the notice as in paragraph 54(3)
may have appeared.

(6) a certified true copy of the resolution referred to in the proviso to the paragraph
54(3) above together with a certified copy of the statement of balance in the
special account from central co-operative bank of the district concerned / state
co-operative bank of the state, as the case may be.

conditions:
If the society has
earmarked government securities and trustee securities for the purpose
requirements.full_sentence:
If the society has
earmarked government securities and trustee securities for the purpose, it
shall provide a certified true copy of the statement furnished by the central co-
operative bank of the district concerned / state co-operative bank of the state,
as the case may be, showing the particulars of government securities and
other trustee securities (and the market value thereof) earmarked and lodged
with the bank for meeting its liability towards the non-member depositors.
requirements.object: copy of the statement furnished by the central co- operative bank of the district concerned / state co-operative bank of the

requirements.full_sentence:
If a society has merely a provision in its by-laws for acceptance of deposits from
anybody but has not accepted any deposits from non-members or has already
refunded whatever deposits it had accepted in the past, it may take steps as
specified in paragraph 54(1), 54(2), and 54(4).
requirements.object: in paragraph 54(1), 54(2), and 54(4)

requirements.full_sentence:
Action as indicated in paragraph
54(3) may not be required.

Similarly, it shall send to the Regional Office (Department
of Regulation) of RBI documents mentioned under paragraph 54(1) and paragraph
54(2) and certified copy of the resolution mentioned in item 54(2) above along with
a certificate to the effect that it has no deposits from non-members on the date of
application.
requirements.object: Office (Department of Regulation) of RBI documents mentioned under paragraph 54(1) and paragraph 54(2) and certified copy of the

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank, other than a salary earners'/employees' co-operative credit society
classified as a primary co-operative bank, which desires to be notified as a non-
banking institution, shall:
(1) amend its by-laws so as to prohibit acceptance of deposits from non-
members;
requirements.object: by-laws so as to prohibit acceptance of deposits from non- members

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that, a bank need not refund the deposits of the members and the
member depositors need not be advised to obtain payment of the balances
standing to credit of their deposit accounts.
requirements.object: not refund the deposits of the members and the member depositors need not be advised to obtain payment of the balances standing to credi
bank is in a position to repay the members' deposits

conditions: if applicable
requirements.full_sentence:
After taking the steps as above and after allowing the period specified for refund of
deposits to lapse, if applicable, the bank intending to go out of the purview of the
Banking Regulation Act (AACS) may apply to the Regional office (Department of
Regulation) of RBI of the state in which the society is situated for notifying it as a
non-banking institution under the provisions of Section 36A(2) of the Banking
Regulation Act (AACS).
requirements.object: office (Department of Regulation) of RBI of the state in which the society is situated for notifying it as a non-banking institu

requirements.full_sentence:
The application shall be accompanied by the following
documents and statements:
<image>
(1) a certified true copy of the amendment of the by-laws prohibiting the
acceptance of deposits from non-members together with a copy of the
certificate of registration of the amendment from the competent authority;
requirements.object: following documents and statements: <image> (1) a certified true copy of the amendment of the by-laws prohibiting the acceptance

requirements.full_sentence:
in which
the notice may have appeared;

and
(6) a certified copy of the statement of balance in the special account from the
central co-operative bank or the state co-operative bank, as the case may be.

conditions:
If the bank/society has earmarked government securities and/or other trustee
securities for the purpose
requirements.full_sentence:
If the bank/society has earmarked government securities and/or other trustee
securities for the purpose, it should furnish a certified true copy of the
statement furnished by the central co-operative bank or the state co-operative
bank showing the particulars of the securities (and the market value thereof)
earmarked and lodged with the bank.
requirements.object: copy of the statement furnished by the central co-operative bank or the state co-operative bank showing the particulars of th

requirements.full_sentence:
After being declared as a non-banking institution the entity may accept deposits from
only its 'members' where 'members' shall mean only full-fledged members of the
society and will not include nominal / associate members.
requirements.object: of the society and will not include nominal / associate members

requirements.full_sentence:
The
Directions, instructions, and guidelines repealed prior to the issuance of these
Directions shall continue to remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken, or
initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall continue to
be governed by the provisions thereof.
requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or acknowledgments granted
under these repealed lists shall be deemed as governed by these
Directions.
requirements.object: by these Directions
affect: (1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder

conditions: if those directions
requirements.full_sentence:
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,
continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may
be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been
repealed.
requirements.object: enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation of
the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being
in force.
requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in force

conditions: if it considers necessary
requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order to
remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of these
Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary clarifications in
respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of any provision of these
Directions given by the RBI shall be final and binding.

requirements.full_sentence: No individual customer/depositor wise details should be furnished.

In the case of any claim for refund of part amount by the depositor whose unclaimed amount/inoperative deposit had been transferred to the Fund, the bank shall claim the entire amount transferred to the Fund in respect of such depositor along with interest payable, if any, from the Fund.
requirements.object: transferred to the Fund in respect of such depositor along with interest payable
